DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF ALAN CLAIR'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT THOMAS MILLER, III'S RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT MILLER SHALL, WITHIN 10 DAYS, SERVE CODE COMPLIANT WRITTEN RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION, TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. FURTHER, THE TRUTH OF THE MATTERS REQUESTED ARE DEEMED ADMITTED. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST DEFENDANT MILLER IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,110. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: TOM COHEN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Mar-07-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance: (via Zoom)
Eunice Chang, Esq. 415-660-3342 for PLaintiff
Tanya Brown 925-705-1017 for Defendant Thomas Miller who is also present
Court Reporter: Liz Lee, CSR #5828, Lizleecsr@att.net, 415-753-3474, Reported.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. Both sides argue. Defendant Thomas Miller is sworn and examined by both sides. Process server Amber Howe is sworn and examined by both sides. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT THOMAS MILLER'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND.

The Court Denies the motion under CCP 425.11. However, due to a clerical error the Court sets aside the default/default judgment. Defendant to file an answer 30 days after notice of entry of order.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Liz Lee, CSR #5828, Lizleecsr@att.net, 415-753-3474, Reported. (501/CFH)

Notice Of Motion To Enter Default Judgment By Court
Off Calendar Sep-01-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Thomas Miller, III.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Eunice F. Chang appeared with plaintiff Alan Clair, who was placed under oath and testified.
Patricia Freytas, a witness for plaintiff, was placed under oath but did not testify.
No appearance by defendant Thomas Miller, III.
Plaintiff's Complaint for Violations of San Francisco Rent Ordinance Section 37.10, et seq. was filed on January 8, 2019, and personally served on defendant Thomas Miller, III on January 22, 2019.
Plaintiff's Statement of Damages was personally served on defendant Thomas Miller, III on December 18, 2018.
Default as to defendant Thomas Miller, III was entered on March 11, 2019.
DOE defendants dismissed today, September 1, 2020.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Alan Clair, and against the defaulted defendant Thomas Miller, III, as follows:

Damages - $330,000.00. ($110,000.00 in damages, trebled per SF Rent Ordinance 37.10, et seq.
Attorney Fees - $15,000.00.
Costs - $749.60.
Sanctions Award Issued 12/11/19 - $1,100.00.

Total - $346,849.60.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.
